abstract_cn: 新兴的自放大攻击（SAA）对域名系统（DNS）构成了严重的拒绝服务攻击威胁。这类攻击能够大幅放大递归DNS服务器与权威DNS服务器之间的交互流量，攻击者只需付出极小的成本就能大量消耗目标服务器的资源。评估此类攻击对全球域名解析基础设施的潜在影响，对于DNS运营管理者有效地进行威胁评估和部署防御措施至关重要，然而这一领域仍然是一片未被充分探索的艰巨领域。本文提出了DaLens，一个大规模描绘DNS自放大攻击威胁全景的系统，通过大规模测量和分析揭示了这一威胁的严重性和普遍性。

title: Inconsistent, Incomplete, and Insecure: A Survey of Account Security Interfaces
authors: Arkaprabha Bhattacharya and Alaa Daffalla, Cornell University; Kevin Lee, Independent Researcher; Rosanna Bellini, New York University; Nicola Dell, Cornell Tech; Thomas Ristenpart, University of Toronto & Cornell Tech
abstract: Despite improvements in account security, compromise remains widespread and damaging, especially when the attacker has close physical or social proximity to the victim (e.g., in terpersonal abuse settings). To help users identify unauthorized access, web services provide account security interfaces (ASIs): notifications and logs that provide information to help infer adversarial compromise. We present the largest measurement study of ASIs to date, evaluating 100 popular services.
abstract_cn: 尽管网络服务的账户安全措施在持续改进，但账户被攻破的事件仍然普遍存在且破坏性巨大，特别是当攻击者与受害者在物理或社交关系上比较接近时（例如家庭暴力或人际虐待场景）。为了帮助用户识别账户是否遭受了未经授权的访问，许多网络服务提供了账户安全界面——即向用户展示通知和访问日志，提供有助于推断是否存在恶意入侵的信息。我们进行了迄今为止规模最大的账户安全界面测量研究，系统评估了100个流行网络服务的账户安全界面设计。

title: Hydrangea: Optimistic Two-Round Partial Synchrony with Improved Fault Resilience
authors: Nibesh Shrestha, Supra Research; Aniket Kate, Supra Research / Purdue University; Kartik Nayak, Duke University
abstract: Consensus protocols in the partially synchronous setting face a fundamental trade-off: achieving optimal Byzantine fault tolerance requires a good-case latency of at least three rounds, while committing in fewer than three rounds generally entails reduced resilience. Even optimistic protocols such as SBFT (DSN'19), FaB (TDSC'06), and Kudzu achieve an optimistic good-case latency of two rounds under favorable conditions, but only at the cost of reduced fault tolerance.
abstract_cn: 在部分同步网络模型下，共识协议面临一个根本性的权衡：要实现最优的拜占庭容错能力，需要至少三轮的消息交换的「好情况」延迟；而在少于三轮的情况下达成共识，通常必须以降低系统的容错能力为代价。即使像SBFT、FaB和Kudzu这样的乐观共识协议，在有利的网络条件下也只能实现两轮的乐观好情况延迟，但这是以降低拜占庭容错能力为代价的。本文提出了Hydrangea，一种改进的乐观两轮部分同步共识协议，在保持两轮好情况延迟的同时，显著提升了系统的容错能力。

title: Revealing the Dark Side of Smart Accounts: An Empirical Study of EIP-7702 Incurred Risks in Blockchain Ecosystem
authors: Mingyuan Huang, Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Han Liu, College of Cryptology and Cyber Science, Nankai University; Shuo Yang, Sun Yat-sen University; Daoyuan Wu, Lingnan University; Shuai Wang, Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
abstract: The introduction of smart accounts by EIP-7702 represents a major advancement for blockchain account abstraction, enabling externally owned accounts (EOAs) to be upgraded into programmable accounts while still preserving their original addresses. This advancement significantly enhances both account functionality and usability, but also redefines blockchain trust boundaries between EOAs and smart contract accounts (CAs), thereby altering security assumptions and creating opportunities for novel types of attack.
abstract_cn: EIP-7702引入的智能账户概念代表了区块链账户抽象技术的重大进步，它使得传统的外部拥有账户（EOA）能够升级为可编程的智能合约账户，同时保留其原有的账户地址。这一进步显著增强了账户的功能性和用户友好性，但同时也重新定义了区块链上EOA与智能合约账户（CA）之间的信任边界，从而从根本上改变了既有的安全假设，并为新型攻击创造了机会。本文对EIP-7702引入的安全风险进行了系统的实证研究，通过大规模数据分析揭示了智能账户在实际部署中面临的各类攻击和安全问题。

title: DMGuard: Safeguarding Kernels from Physical-Page Use-After-Free Vulnerabilities
authors: Juhee Kim, Jaeyoung Chung, Dae R. Jeong, and Byoungyoung Lee, Seoul National University
abstract: This paper is currently under embargo, but the paper abstract is available now. The final paper PDF will be available on the first day of the conference.
abstract_cn: 本文目前处于embargo状态，但论文摘要现已可用。最终论文PDF将在会议第一天提供。

title: Garuda and Pari: Faster and Smaller SNARKs via Equifficient Polynomial Commitments
authors: Michel Dellepere, Independent; Pratyush Mishra and Alireza Shirzad, University of Pennsylvania
abstract: SNARKs are powerful cryptographic primitives that allow a prover to produce a succinct proof of a computation. Two key goals of SNARK research are to minimize the size of the proof and to minimize the time required to generate it. In this work, we present new SNARK constructions that push the frontier on both of these goals.
abstract_cn: SNARK（简洁非交互式知识论证）是强大的密码学原语，它允许证明者为任意计算生成一个简短的证明，并且验证者可以高效地验证该证明的正确性。SNARK研究的两个核心目标是最小化生成的证明的大小和最小化生成证明所需的计算时间。在这项工作中，我们提出了名为Garuda和Pari的新型SNARK构造方案，在这两个关键目标上同时推动了前沿技术的发展，显著改进了现有方案的性能。

title: Transparent Dictionaries from Polynomial Commitments
authors: Hossein Hafezi, NYU; Alireza Shirzad, University of Pennsylvania; Benedikt Bünz and Joseph Bonneau, NYU
abstract: We present IRONDICT, a transparent dictionary construction based on polynomial commitment schemes. Transparent dictionaries enable an untrusted server to maintain a mutable dictionary and provably serve clients lookup queries. A major open challenge is supporting efficient auditing by lightweight clients. Previous solutions either incurred high server costs (limiting throughput) or high client lookup verification costs, hindering them from modern messaging key transparency deployments with billions of users. Our construction makes black-box use of a generic multilinear polynomial commitment scheme and inherits its security notions, i.e. binding and zero-knowledge. We implement our construction with the recent KZH scheme and find that a dictionary with 1 billion entries can be verified on a consumer-grade laptop in 35 milliseconds, a 300 -fold improvement over the state of the art. Our construction also offers 150000x smaller proofs ( 8 Kilobytes) and perfect privacy, with concretely efficient client and server costs. We also show fast-forwarding techniques based on incremental verifiable computation (IVC) and checkpoints to enable even faster client auditing.
abstract_cn: 我们提出了IRONDICT，一种基于多项式承诺方案的透明字典构造。透明字典允许一个不可信的服务器维护一个可变的数据字典，并向客户端可靠地提供查找查询服务，同时客户端可以高效地验证服务器返回结果的正确性。一个主要的开放性挑战是如何支持轻量级客户端的高效审计能力。先前的解决方案要么导致服务器端的计算成本过高（限制了系统的吞吐量），要么导致客户端验证查询的时间过长，这些都阻碍了它们在拥有数十亿用户的现代消息密钥透明度系统中的应用。我们的构造黑盒式地使用通用的多线性多项式承诺方案，并继承了其安全性概念。我们用近期的KZH方案实现了我们的构造。

title: Efficient Threshold ML-DSA
authors: Sofía Celi, Brave Research; Rafael del Pino and Thomas Espitau, PQShield; Guilhem Niot, PQShield and Univ Rennes, CNRS, IRISA; Thomas Prest, PQShield
abstract: Threshold signature schemes allow a group of users to jointly generate a digital signature, providing resilience against faults and enhancing decentralization. With the advent of post-quantum cryptography, lattice-based threshold signatures have gained attention as viable alternatives. Nevertheless, existing constructions frequently encounter challenges related to scalability, robustness, or compatibility with standardized schemes, particularly with the NIST-selected and standardised Module-Lattice-based Digital Signature Algorithm (ML-DSA) algorithm.
abstract_cn: 门限签名方案允许多个用户共同协作生成一个有效的数字签名，这提供了对部分节点故障的容错能力并增强了系统的去中心化程度。随着后量子密码学时代的到来，基于格的密码学方案逐渐成熟，基于格的门限签名作为传统门限签名的可行替代方案受到了广泛关注。然而，现有的构造在可扩展性、鲁棒性或与现有标准化签名方案的兼容性方面经常遇到重大挑战，特别是与NIST选定并标准化的模块格数字签名算法（ML-DSA）的兼容性问题尤为突出。本文提出了高效的阈值ML-DSA方案。

title: Nudge: A Private Recommendations Engine
authors: Alexandra Henzinger, MIT; Emma Dauterman, MIT and Stanford; Henry Corrigan-Gibbs, MIT; Dan Boneh, Stanford
abstract: Nudge is a recommender system with cryptographic privacy. A Nudge deployment consists of three infrastructure servers and many users, who retrieve/rate items from a large data set (e.g., videos, posts, businesses). Periodically, the Nudge servers collect ratings from users in secret-shared form, then run a three-party computation to train a lightweight recommender model on users' private ratings. Finally, the servers deliver personalized recommendations to each user. At every step, Nudge reveals nothing to the servers about any user's preferences beyond the aggregate model itself. User privacy holds against an adversary that compromises the entire secret state of one server. The technical core of Nudge is a new, three-party protocol for matrix factorization. On the Netflix data set with half a million users and ten thousand items, Nudge (running on three 192-core servers on a local-area network) privately learns a recommender model in 50 mins with 40 GB of server-to-server communication. On a standard quality benchmark (nDCG@20), Nudge scores 0.29 out of 1.0, on par with non-private matrix factorization and just shy of non-private neural recommenders, which score 0.31.
abstract_cn: Nudge是一个具有密码学隐私保护的推荐系统。一个Nudge部署由三台基础设施服务器和大量的用户组成，用户可以从一个大型的数据集（例如视频、帖子、商家信息）中检索和评分项目。周期性地，Nudge服务器以秘密共享的形式从用户那里收集评分数据，然后运行一个三方安全计算协议，在用户的私有评分数据上训练一个轻量级的推荐模型，整个过程不会泄露任何单个用户的评分信息。

title: On Evaluating the Robustness of Large Vision-Language Models via Untargeted Modality Alignment Breaking Adversarial Attack
authors: Zhichao Li, Hongshan Yang, Zhibo Wang, Huiyu Xu, and Junhong Lai, Zhejiang University; Yaopeng Wang, Southeastern University and Zhejiang University; Kui Ren and Chun Chen, Zhejiang University
abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have achieved remarkable success in multimodal tasks by aligning the representation space of visual encoders to that of the LLMs. However, they remain vulnerable to transferable adversarial attacks, which can manipulate the LVLMs' output without accessing the model. Ensuring their reliable deployment thus requires a rigorous evaluation of black-box robustness. Current methods provide a limited assessment by perturbing only the visual encoder of LVLMs and often neglect untargeted attack scenarios. In this work, we propose the Modality Alignment Breaking Attack (MABA), a novel transferable, untargeted adversarial attack for evaluating the black-box robustness of LVLMs. MABA emphasizes disrupting the entire multimodal pipeline, targeting two key phases: visual encoding and modality alignment. First, MABA reveals that the core of transferable adversarial attacks lies in suppressing discriminative visual representations and explicitly uses this as an optimization objective to improve transferability across different LVLMs. Second, MABA introduces a mutual-information-aware projector that acts as a surrogate modality alignment module of LVLMs, effectively breaking cross-modal consistency and enhancing the transferability. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that MABA achieves state-of-the-art performance, leading to an average 58.37% drop in semantic metrics for the image caption task. Through ablation studies on diverse LVLM families, we derive valuable insights into strengthening the robustness of LVLMs.
abstract_cn: 大型视觉语言模型通过将视觉编码器的表示空间与大型语言模型的表示空间进行对齐，在多模态理解任务中取得了显著成功。然而，它们仍然容易受到可迁移的对抗性攻击的影响——这些攻击可以在不访问模型内部参数的黑盒条件下操纵大型视觉语言模型的输出，使其生成错误描述、有害内容或拒绝执行指令。确保大型视觉语言模型的可靠部署需要对其黑盒鲁棒性进行严格的评估。本文通过系统性的实验评估了大型视觉语言模型在一种新型黑盒攻击方式——未经目标模态对齐破坏（Untargeted Modality Alignment Breaking）攻击下的鲁棒性表现，该攻击通过精心设计对抗性扰动来破坏视觉与语言模态之间的语义对齐，从而在不直接修改视觉内容的情况下绕过模型的安全机制。

title: From Easy to Hard++: Promoting Differentially Private Image Synthesis Through Spatial-Frequency Curriculum
authors: Chen GONG and Kecen Li, University of Virginia; Zinan Lin, Microsoft Research; Tianhao Wang, University of Virginia
abstract: Differentially private (DP) synthetic images serve as a critical tool for reducing privacy concerns by mimicking the statistical properties of sensitive data while ensuring privacy guarantees. To improve the quality of synthetic images, most studies have focused on improving the core optimization techniques (e.g., DP-SGD). Recently, we have witnessed a paradigm shift that takes these techniques off the shelf and studies how to use them together to achieve the best results. One notable work is DP-FETA, which proposes using 'central images' for 'warming up' the DP training and then using traditional DP-SGD.
abstract_cn: 差分隐私合成图像通过模拟真实敏感数据的统计分布特性同时提供可量化的隐私保证，已成为降低隐私担忧的关键技术工具。为了提高合成图像的质量，大多数研究集中在改进核心的优化训练技术（例如差分隐私随机梯度下降）。最近，我们见证了一种范式转变，即将这些优化技术应用于更实际和更具挑战性的场景中。本文提出了从简单到困难逐步推进的差分隐私图像合成方法，系统地提升了合成图像的质量。

title: FIRA: Enabling Automatic Forensic Investigation of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Yizhi Huang, Georgia Institute of Technology; David Oygenblik, Georgia Tech; Runze Zhang, Mingxuan Yao, Muhammad Ibrahim, Burak Sahin, Haichuan Xu, Saman Zonouz, and Brendan Saltaformaggio, Georgia Institute of Technology
abstract: In dynamic environments, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) often utilize online learning to refine their machine learning (ML) model's decision boundaries for improved performance. Unfortunately, when the UAV becomes irrecoverable or unavailable (e.g., a crash), a forensic investigator would be left helpless to determine if the UAV's online learning caused the crash. This paper proposes a novel forensic technique, called FIRA, that can establish causal connections from ML models to UAV system components. FIRA sends back in-flight online learning updates and telemetry data (even when bandwidth is limited) and determines whether the crash can be attributed to the online learning model. We applied FIRA to 48 UAV crash scenarios using two widely adopted UAV control programs: PX4 and ArduPilot. Across four types of UAV missions, FIRA investigated 12 accidents (each) in which a backdoored online learning model was the cause of the crash, and FIRA was able to correctly attribute the model to the crash with 95.8% accuracy.
abstract_cn: 在动态变化的运行环境中，无人机常常利用在线学习技术来持续优化其机器学习模型的决策边界，以提升在变化环境中的任务性能。然而，当无人机变得不可恢复或完全不可用（例如发生坠毁）时，事故调查人员将无法判断无人机的在线学习过程是否直接导致了坠毁事故的发生。本文提出了一个名为FIRA的新型取证分析框架，能够对无人机进行自动化的数字取证调查，分析在线学习过程与事故之间的因果关系。

title: MASLeak: Investigating and Exposing Intellectual Property Leakage Vulnerabilities in Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Liwen Wang, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Wenxuan Wang, Renmin University of China; Shuai Wang, Zongjie Li, Zhenlan Ji, and Zongyi LYU, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Daoyuan Wu, Lingnan University; Shing-Chi Cheung, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
abstract: The rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) has led to the emergence of Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) to perform complex tasks through collaboration. However, the intricate nature of MAS, including their architecture, agent interactions, and complex internal communication processing, raises significant concerns regarding intellectual property (IP) protection. In this paper, we introduce MASLEAK, the first framework for systematically extracting IP from MAS in a practical black-box setting. We assume a realistic adversary who can only submit queries to the system's public API and observe the final output, without any prior knowledge of the internal architecture and the backend LLM information. Inspired by how computer worms propagate and infect vulnerable network hosts, MASLEAK carefully crafts adversarial query q to elicit, propagate, and retain responses from each MAS agent that reveal a full set of proprietary components, including the number of agents, topology, system prompts, task instructions, and tool usages. We construct the first synthetic dataset of 810 MAS applications and also evaluate MASLEAK against real-world MAS applications, including Coze and CrewAI. MASLEAK achieves high accuracy in extracting MAS IP, with an average attack success rate of 87% for system prompts and task instructions, and 92% for system architecture in most cases. We conclude by discussing the implications of our findings and the potential defenses.
abstract_cn: 大型语言模型的快速发展催生了多智能体系统的兴起——通过多个专门化的智能体之间的分工协作来完成复杂任务。然而，多智能体系统的复杂性——包括其分层架构设计、智能体之间的交互模式以及复杂的内部通信处理机制——引发了对知识产权保护的重大关切。在本文中，我们介绍了MASLeak，对多智能体系统中存在的知识产权泄露漏洞进行了系统性的调查和曝光。我们的研究揭示了通过分析智能体间的通信模式和响应行为，攻击者可以窃取专有的算法设计、商业敏感信息以及训练数据中的机密内容。

title: Love, Lies, and Language Models: Investigating AI's Role in Romance-Baiting Scams
authors: Gilad Gressel and Rahul Pankajakshan, Center for Cybersecurity Systems & Networks, Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Amritapuri; Shir Rozenfeld, Ben Gurion University of the Negev; Ling Li, Ca' Foscari University of Venice; Ivan Franceschini, University of Melbourne; Krishnashree Achuthan, Center for Cybersecurity Systems & Networks, Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Amritapuri; Yisroel Mirsky, Ben Gurion University of the Negev
abstract: Romance-baiting scams have become a major source of financial and emotional harm worldwide. These operations are run by organized crime syndicates that traffic thousands of people into forced labor, requiring them to build emotional intimacy with victims over weeks of text conversations before pressuring them into fraudulent cryptocurrency investments. Because the scams are inherently text-based, they raise urgent questions about the role of Large Language Models (LLMs) in both current and future automation.
abstract_cn: 爱情诱饵诈骗已成为全球范围内造成重大财务和情感伤害的主要来源之一。这些诈骗活动由有组织的犯罪集团运营，他们贩卖数千人进行强迫劳动，要求这些受害者通过数周的密集文本对话与目标建立情感亲密关系，然后逐步施压迫使他们进行欺诈性的加密货币投资。由于这些诈骗本质上完全基于文本交互，大型语言模型的能力进步可能会显著影响此类诈骗的实施难易程度和规模化程度。本文深入调查了AI在爱情诱饵诈骗中扮演的角色以及潜在的影响。

title: United We Defend: Collaborative Membership Inference Defenses in Federated Learning
authors: Li Bai, Junxu Liu, Sen Zhang, Xinwei Zhang, Qingqing Ye, and Haibo Hu, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University
abstract: Membership inference attacks (MIAs), which determine whether a specific data point was included in the training set of a target model, have posed severe threats in federated learning (FL). Unfortunately, existing MIA defenses, typically applied independently to each client in FL, are ineffective against powerful trajectory-based MIAs that exploit temporal information throughout the training process to infer membership status. In this paper, we investigate a new FL defense scenario driven by heterogeneous privacy needs and privacy-utility trade-offs, where only a subset of clients are defended, as well as a collaborative defense mode where clients cooperate to mitigate membership privacy leakage. To this end, we introduce CoFedMID, a collaborative defense framework against MIAs in FL, which limits local model memorization of training samples and, through a defender coalition, enhances privacy protection and model utility. Specifically, CoFedMID consists of three modules: a class-guided partition module for selective local training samples, a utility-aware compensation module to recycle contributive samples and prevent their overconfidence, and an aggregation-neutral perturbation module that injects noise for cancellation at the coalition level into client updates. Extensive experiments on three datasets show that our defense framework significantly reduces the performance of seven MIAs while incurring only a small utility loss. These results are consistently verified across various defense settings.
abstract_cn: 成员推理攻击通过判断某个特定的数据点是否被用于训练目标模型，对联邦学习中的用户数据隐私构成了严重威胁。在这种攻击中，攻击者（通常是联邦学习中的参数服务器）通过观察客户端上传的模型更新（梯度或参数）来推断该客户端的训练数据中是否包含某个特定的记录。不幸的是，现有的成员推理攻击防御方法（如差分隐私、梯度压缩等）通常是在联邦学习过程中独立地应用于每个客户端，对于利用整个训练过程中时间维度信息的强大的基于轨迹的成员推理攻击效果不佳——这类攻击通过追踪模型参数在多次训练迭代中的变化轨迹来更准确地推断成员关系。本文提出了一种协作式的联合防御方法，让联邦学习中的各个参与方协同工作，共同抵御基于轨迹的高级成员推理攻击。

title: Tracegram: Framing Trace-Level Traffic Analysis with Temporally-Aware Multiple Instance Learning
authors: Jian Qu, Yuchen Zhang, Jialong Zhang, Jianfeng Li, and Xiaobo Ma, School of Computer Science and Technology, Xi'an Jiaotong University
abstract: Modern network behaviors span multiple flows and evolve over time, making temporal and co-occurrence contexts across flows essential for reliable traffic analysis. This need is especially critical in the security domain, where attacks progress through reconnaissance, delivery, command and control, and lateral movement over extended intervals and across multiple flows. Existing packet-level or single-flow approaches fragment this context and limit performance on trace-level classification, detection, and attribution. We introduce the trace as the analysis unit and present Tracegram, which formulates trace-level analysis as Multiple Instance Learning. Tracegram combines per-flow encoders with a temporally aware aggregation module to reason across flows, preserve long-range dependencies, and produce key-flow attribution signals that support analyst verification and forensics. Our validation spans theory and practice. We theoretically justify the MIL-based decomposition for trace-level traffic analysis and conduct extensive experiments on four public datasets across multiple tasks, showing better or comparable performance to state-of-the-art methods. Finally, case studies on APT traces from the DAPT dataset show that Tracegram highlights flows aligned with attack phases, enabling targeted investigation.
abstract_cn: 现代网络的行为模式跨越多个网络流并随时间动态演变，这使得跨流的时序上下文和共现上下文信息对于可靠的网络流量分析至关重要。这种需求在网络安全领域尤其关键，因为复杂的网络攻击会通过侦察、武器投递、命令与控制以及横向移动等多个阶段，在较长的时间跨度和多个网络流中逐步推进。现有的基于单个数据包或单个流的分析方法无法有效捕获这些复杂的时空依赖关系。本文提出了Tracegram，一种利用时序感知的多实例学习方法进行轨迹级细粒度流量分析的新型框架。

title: "Abuse Risks are Often Inherent to Product Features": Exploring AI Vendors' Bug Bounty and Responsible Disclosure Policies
authors: Yangheran Piao, Jingjie Li, and Daniel W. Woods, University of Edinburgh
abstract: As vendors adopt AI technologies, security researchers are working to uncover and fix related vulnerabilities, which is important given AI systems handle sensitive data and critical functions. This process relies on vendors receiving and rewarding AI vulnerability reports. To assess current practices, we analyzed the vulnerability disclosure policies of 264 AI vendors. We employed a mixed-methods approach, combining snapshot and longitudinal qualitative analysis, as well as comparing alignment with 320 AI incidents and 260 academic articles. Our analysis reveals that 36% of AI vendors have no established policy, and only 18% mention AI risks. Data access, authorization, and model extraction vulnerabilities are most consistently declared in-scope. Jailbreaking and hallucination are most commonly declared out-of-scope. We identify three profiles that reflect vendors' different positions toward AI vulnerabilities: proactive clarification (n = 46), silent (n = 115), and restrictive (n = 103). Our alignment results suggest that vendors may address AI vulnerability disclosure later than academic research and real-world incidents.
abstract_cn: 随着越来越多的供应商采用人工智能技术，安全研究人员正在努力发现和修复AI系统中存在的安全漏洞，鉴于AI系统通常处理敏感数据并承担关键功能，这项工作尤为重要。这一过程依赖于供应商能够接收并妥善奖励AI安全漏洞报告。为了评估当前的行业实践状况，我们分析了264家AI供应商的漏洞披露政策。我们采用了混合研究方法，既对公开的漏洞披露政策内容进行系统分析，也通过问卷调查了解安全研究人员在向AI供应商报告漏洞时的实际体验。

title: PICS: Private Intersection over Committed (and reusable) Sets
authors: Aarushi Goel, Rutgers University; Peihan Miao and Phuoc Van Long Pham, Brown University; Satvinder Singh, Purdue University
abstract: Private Set Intersection (PSI) enables two parties to compute the intersection of their private sets without revealing any additional information. While maliciously secure PSI protocols prevent many attacks, adversaries can still exploit them by using inconsistent inputs across multiple sessions. This limitation stems from the definition of malicious security in secure multiparty computation, but is particularly problematic in PSI because: (1) real-world applications—such as Apple's PSI protocol for CSAM detection and private contact discovery in messaging apps—often require multiple PSI executions over consistent inputs, and (2) the PSI functionality makes it relatively easy for adversaries to infer additional information.
abstract_cn: 私密集交集协议使两个参与方能够计算出各自私有数据集合的交集，而不会泄露交集之外的任何额外信息。虽然现有的恶意安全私密集交集协议能够防止多种攻击，但攻击者仍然可以通过在不同的协议执行会话中使用不一致的输入数据来利用这些协议。这种局限性的根源在于安全多方计算中恶意安全性的定义方式，但在实际场景中仍然构成了真实的安全威胁。本文提出了PICS，一种基于承诺（且可重用）集合的私有交集协议，有效解决了输入不一致的问题。

title: MULCOTAINT: Towards Efficient Multi-tag Dynamic Taint Analysis via Hardware/Software Co-design
authors: Bing Qi, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences; Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Yi Yang and Xiangkun Jia, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences; Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Key Laboratory of System Software (Chinese Academy of Sciences); Zhengpin Qian and Huafeng Huang, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences; Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Purui Su, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences; Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Key Laboratory of System Software (Chinese Academy of Sciences)
abstract: Multi-tag dynamic taint analysis (M-DTA) is critical in fine-grained analysis scenarios such as vulnerability analysis. However, current software solutions have serious performance problems. Although hardware solutions are promising, they are single-tag and difficult to extend to M-DTA. We propose an efficient M-DTA framework named MULCOTAINT via hardware/software co-design. We decouple the taint analysis from the normal execution with the coprocessor architecture and solve several challenges, such as designing taint calculation as vectorized calculation, managing taint tags with page tables, and providing functionality interfaces of the taint analysis engine. We build a dataset of 32 programs with 5 types and conduct the performance evaluation and vulnerability analysis experiments. The results show that MULCOTAINT has high performance and acceptable memory usage with abilities of detailed vulnerability analysis. MULCOTAINT outperforms the software solutions (TaintRabbit and PANDA) and hardware solutions (HardTaint, RAFT, and FineDIFT). The maximum difference of overhead increase based on the respective baselines could be '1.14x vs. 4409.09x' for 'MULCOTAINT vs. PANDA', while HardTaint's average overhead increase is 19.57 times that of MULCOTAINT. Although the prototype of MULCOTAINT's hardware cost is higher than embedded-oriented works RAFT and FineDIFT, it is acceptable due to M-DTA's complex logic.
abstract_cn: 多标签动态污点分析在漏洞分析等细粒度的安全分析场景中发挥着至关重要的作用。然而，当前基于纯软件实现的多标签动态污点分析方案存在严重的性能问题，无法满足实际应用的需求。虽然基于硬件加速的方案在性能上很有前景，但它们主要支持单标签污点分析，难以扩展到多标签场景。本文通过硬件/软件协同设计的方法，提出了一个高效的多标签动态污点分析框架，命名为MULCOTAINT。我们创新地将污点跟踪过程与污点分析过程进行了解耦，从而实现了显著的性能加速。

title: WAVED: Principled Identification of Off-Path Exploitable Weak Verifications within the TCP/IP Protocol Suite
authors: Yizhou Zhao and Xuewei Feng, Tsinghua University; Min Li, Zhongguancun Laboratory; Ke Xu, Tsinghua University
abstract: Off-path exploits targeting the fundamental TCP/IP protocol suite pose significant threats to the security of the Internet infrastructure. In particular, weak verifications of received payloads—arising from the lack of reliable information to validate or implementation flaws within the suite—lead to vulnerabilities that attackers can exploit to manipulate traffic, induce data loss, and disrupt services on victim servers. In this paper, we present the first systematic study of these vulnerabilities and introduce WAVED, a framework for identifying off-path exploitable weak verifications within the TCP/IP protocol suite implementation. At the core of WAVED, we develop a flow-, context-, and field-sensitive pointer analysis tailored to the TCP/IP kernel, and construct a Taint Propagation Graph (TPG) to model and trace data flow within the stack. By modeling byte-granularity taint propagation across diverse arithmetic operations, our approach can accurately locate specific input bytes associated with each constraint. Furthermore, direction-sensitive taint information is computed to accurately capture and differentiate the strength of constraints imposed by alternative branch outcomes, thereby significantly outperforming traditional byte-insensitive and direction-insensitive analyses. We evaluate WAVED on IPv4 and IPv6 across Linux 5.15, Linux 6.8, and FreeBSD 14.1. It precisely uncovers weak verifications leading to semantic vulnerabilities in TCP/IP and reveals 14 previously unknown vulnerabilities. We have responsibly disclosed these vulnerabilities to the affected OS vendors and have received acknowledgments from the Linux community.
abstract_cn: 针对基础TCP/IP协议族的离路网络攻击——即攻击者不能直接观察或修改通信双方之间的数据包，只能通过发送伪造的数据包来干扰通信——对互联网基础设施的安全构成了重大威胁。特别是，由于协议族内缺乏可靠的信息来验证接收到的数据包负载的有效性，或者由于具体实现中的缺陷，导致的负载验证薄弱——这些验证漏洞源于协议规范中的内在歧义和不同操作系统实现之间的不一致——使得攻击者能够成功注入恶意负载，进而操纵网络流量、导致数据丢失以及中断正常的网络服务。本文提出了WAVED，一种基于原则的系统化方法，用于识别TCP/IP协议族中可能被离路攻击利用的弱验证条件或漏洞，覆盖了从TCP到UDP、从IPv4到IPv6的多种协议场景。

title: CuSafe: Capturing Memory Corruption on NVIDIA GPUs
authors: Hongyi Lu, Southern University of Science and Technology and Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Fengwei Zhang, Southern University of Science and Technology; Zhenkai Zhang, Clemson University; Shuai Wang, Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Yanan Guo, University of Rochester
abstract: Modern GPU applications, particularly in machine learning and scientific computing, are increasingly affected by memory corruption bugs due to their reliance on memory-unsafe languages like C/C++. However, existing solutions either depend on hardware/software that is not available on commodity GPUs, or incur prohibitive performance overheads, rendering them impractical for real-world deployment.
abstract_cn: 现代GPU应用程序，特别是在机器学习和科学计算领域，由于广泛依赖C/C++等内存不安全的编程语言，正越来越多地受到内存损坏错误的影响。然而，现有的GPU内存安全解决方案要么依赖于商用GPU上不可用的特殊硬件或软件支持，要么引入过于高昂的性能开销，使得它们无法在实际部署中使用。本文提出了CuSafe，一种专门针对NVIDIA GPU的运行时内存损坏检测和捕获方案，能够以合理的开销有效地检测GPU程序中的内存安全错误。

title: Attacks on Approximate Caches in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
authors: Desen Sun, Shuncheng Jie, and Sihang Liu, University of Waterloo
abstract: Diffusion models are a powerful class of generative models that produce images and other content from user prompts, but they are computationally intensive. To mitigate this cost, recent academic and industry work has adopted approximate caching, which reuses intermediate states from similar prompts in a cache. While efficient, this optimization introduces new security risks by breaking isolation among users. This paper provides a comprehensive assessment of the security vulnerabilities introduced by approximate caching. First, we demonstrate a remote covert channel established with the approximate cache, where a sender injects prompts with special keywords into the cache system and a receiver can recover that even after days, to exchange information. Second, we introduce a prompt stealing attack using the approximate cache, where an attacker can recover existing cached prompts from hits. Finally, we introduce a poisoning attack that embeds the attacker's logos into the previously stolen prompt, leading to unexpected logo rendering for the requests that hit the poisoned cache prompts. These attacks are all performed remotely through the serving system, demonstrating severe security vulnerabilities in approximate caching. The code for this work is available.
abstract_cn: 扩散模型是一类强大的生成式模型，能够根据用户输入的提示词生成高质量的图像和其他内容，但这类模型的计算量极其巨大。为了降低计算成本，近期的学术和工业界工作广泛采用了近似缓存技术——即在缓存中重用来自相似或相关提示词的中间计算结果（即扩散过程中的中间状态）。虽然这种优化显著提升了推理效率，但它通过打破不同提示词请求之间的执行隔离引入了新的安全风险：一个用户可以通过精心设计的提示词影响缓存状态，从而影响另一个用户的生成结果。本文系统性地研究了对文本到图像扩散模型中近似缓存机制的各类攻击方法。

title: Side-Channel Attacks on Open vSwitch
authors: Daewoo Kim and Sihang Liu, University of Waterloo
abstract: Virtualization is widely adopted in cloud systems to manage resource sharing among users. A virtualized environment usually deploys a virtual switch within the host system to enable virtual machines to communicate with each other and with the physical network. The Open vSwitch (OVS) is one of the most popular software-based virtual switches. It maintains a cache hierarchy to accelerate packet forwarding from the host to virtual machines. We characterize the caching system inside OVS from a security perspective and identify three attack primitives. Based on the attack primitives, we present three remote attacks via OVS, breaking the isolation in virtualized environments. First, we identify remote covert channels using different caches. Second, we present a novel header recovery attack that leaks a remote user's packet header fields, breaking the confidentiality guarantees from the system. Third, we demonstrate a remote packet rate monitoring attack that recovers the packet rate of a remote victim. To defend against these attacks, we also discuss potential mitigations.
abstract_cn: 虚拟化技术在云计算系统中被广泛采用，用于管理和隔离不同用户之间的资源共享。一个虚拟化环境通常会在宿主操作系统中部署一个虚拟交换机，使运行在宿主机上的各个虚拟机能够相互通信以及与外部物理网络进行通信。Open vSwitch（OVS）是目前最流行的基于软件的虚拟交换机之一。它内部维护了一个多级缓存层次结构来加速数据包的转发处理。本文研究了针对Open vSwitch缓存系统的侧信道攻击方法。

title: A Distortion-minimization Watermarking Framework for Large Language Models: Larger Capacity, Stronger Robustness and Higher Quality
authors: Liming Zhai, Xuezhou Shang, Liyun Zhang, and Po Hu, Central China Normal University
abstract: Large language model (LLM) watermarking provides verifiable source identification for generated text, and its practical deployment requires large watermark capacity, strong robustness against attacks, and high text quality. However, existing methods often struggle to balance all these criteria, typically addressing them with separate designs. To overcome this, we propose a distortion-minimization watermarking (DMW) framework that unifies capacity, robustness and quality within a single optimization paradigm. This framework models robustness and quality as distortion costs for text modifications, minimizing the total distortion for a given watermark length to achieve an optimal trade-off. Specifically, we design several distortion costs: a robustness cost leveraging semantic invariance to resist attacks, and two quality costs guiding modifications toward low-cohesion, high-variability regions to reduce perceptual impact. We then propose periodically optimized syndrome-trellis codes (PO-STCs), formulating overall distortion minimization as a periodic shortest-path problem. This enables real-time optimization for sequential generation with flexible capacity control. Extensive experiments across diverse datasets and LLMs demonstrate DMW's superiority, outperforming state-of-the-art methods across all criteria. Notably, under severe paraphrasing attacks, DMW achieves a match rate up to 46.35% higher than the best baseline, while maintaining superior text quality.
abstract_cn: 大型语言模型水印技术为生成的文本内容提供了可验证的来源追溯能力——即可以证明某段文本是由特定模型生成的。实际部署中，水印方案需要同时具备大的信息容量（能嵌入足够多的信息）、对各种攻击的强鲁棒性以及生成文本的高质量。然而，现有的水印方法通常难以同时满足所有这些要求，往往只能针对某一两个方面进行单独优化设计，导致在其他方面表现不佳。为了解决这一挑战，我们提出了一个面向大型语言模型的、以最小化生成文本失真为核心目标的水印框架，在保证大容量和强鲁棒性的同时最大程度地保持生成文本的自然流畅性和质量。

title: Behind Bars: A Side-Channel Attack on NVIDIA MIG Cache Partitioning Using Memory Barriers
authors: Cheng Gu, University of Rochester; Reese Levine, UC Santa Cruz; Zhenkai Zhang, Clemson University; Tyler Sorensen, Microsoft and UC Santa Cruz; Yanan Guo, University of Rochester
abstract: NVIDIA Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) is a feature designed to enable isolation and secure multi-tenancy on large data center GPUs. MIG partitions a single GPU into multiple instances, each with dedicated hardware resources such as L2 cache slices. MIG is also documented to form the foundation of NVIDIA's confidential computing stack by providing hardware-isolated trusted execution environments. However, the security claims of MIG deserve closer investigation, especially given the complexity of the GPU memory system and its many (sparsely documented) memory instructions.
abstract_cn: NVIDIA的多实例GPU（MIG）是一项专门设计用于在大型数据中心GPU上实现严格的资源隔离和安全多租户的功能。MIG技术将单个物理GPU划分为多个独立的GPU实例，每个实例都拥有专用的硬件资源，例如独立的L2缓存切片、内存控制器和计算单元。MIG还被官方文档描述为NVIDIA机密计算技术栈的基础，通过提供硬件级别的隔离来构建可信执行环境。然而，本文提出了Behind Bars攻击，一种利用内存屏障指令对NVIDIA MIG的缓存隔离机制进行侧信道攻击的方法，证明了即使在MIG隔离下，不同实例之间的信息泄露仍然是可能的。

title: TrojPix: Electromagnetic Covert Channels via Imperceptible Pixel Modulation
authors: Guoming Zhang, Shandong University and Quan Cheng Laboratory; Huiting Zhang, Zhenwei Lu, Heqiang Fu, Xin Gao, Riccardo Spolaor, and Yetong Cao, Shandong University; Yanni Yang and Pengfei Hu, Shandong University and Quan Cheng Laboratory
abstract: Air-gapped networks rely on physical isolation to prevent external connectivity. Prior electromagnetic (EM) covert channels have exploited emissions from video cables, memory buses, and CPUs, yet they rarely achieve high throughput, long range, and visual imperceptibility simultaneously, limiting practical utility in air-gapped settings. We show that imperceptible pixel modulation can deterministically induce controllable EM emissions on digital video cables, enabling control without system privileges or hardware modifications. Building on this insight, we present TrojPix, a covert channel that maintains on-screen imperceptibility while delivering high-speed, long-range communication over digital video cables. We realize a lightweight communication scheme that combines pixel-to-sample mapping with adaptive decoding, enabling sample-rate-level robust communication over extended ranges. We evaluate TrojPix across nine commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) monitor manufacturers and fifteen COTS digital video cables under realistic conditions, demonstrating its effectiveness in two attack modes: fake screen-off and foreground embedding. TrojPix achieves a peak throughput of 8.1 Mbps and a maximum range of 208 m, revealing a practical and stealthy threat to the security of air-gapped networks.
abstract_cn: 气隙网络（Air-gapped networks）通过物理隔离的方式完全断开与外部网络的连接，以防止网络攻击。先前的电磁隐蔽信道研究利用了视频线缆、内存总线和CPU等组件的电磁辐射来进行数据泄露，但这些方法很少能同时实现高传输速率、远距离通信和视觉不可感知性，这严重限制了它们在气隙环境中的实际应用价值。我们证明了通过人眼不可感知的像素级屏幕亮度调制，可以确定性地控制来自HDMI和DisplayPort接口的电磁辐射信号。本文提出了TrojPix，一种通过不可感知的像素调制实现的电磁隐蔽信道攻击方法。

title: Anonymous Tokens with Designated-Reader Metadata Bit
authors: Aisha Tu, Wuhan University; Meng Jia, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University; Kun He, Jing Chen, and Ruiying Du, Wuhan University
abstract: Anonymous tokens with private metadata bit convey hidden signals to verifiers when presented by the user and are under discussion in standardization. Existing solutions only allow the token issuer to read the signals, which places a heavy burden on the issuer and makes it challenging to support issuer-hiding because verifiers have to contact the issuer. In this paper, we propose an anonymous token scheme with designated-reader metadata bit, allowing the user to specify an issuer-accepted verifier to read the signal from the token directly. We also extend our scheme to support reader-hiding, which conceals the user's intended verifier from the issuer and other verifiers, and issuer-hiding, which prevents exposure of the token issuer from verifiers. We prove the security of our constructions and report their performance.
abstract_cn: 具有私有元数据位（private metadata bit）的匿名令牌在用户向验证者出示令牌时传递隐藏的信号，目前正在相关的标准化组织中进行讨论。现有的技术方案只允许令牌的发行者能够读取这个隐藏信号位，这给发行者带来了沉重的计算和通信负担，而且因为验证者必须在线联系发行者才能验证令牌，使得支持「发行者隐藏」（issuer-hiding）变得非常困难。在本文中，我们提出了一种新型的匿名令牌方案，支持指定读者（designated-reader）可以读取元数据位，解决了上述问题。

title: Paper Title Under Embargo
authors: Author list under embargo.
abstract: This paper is currently under embargo. The final paper PDF and abstract will be available on the first day of the conference.
abstract_cn: 本文目前处于embargo状态。最终论文PDF和摘要将在会议第一天提供。

title: TIMESLICE-SANDWICH: A GPU Side-Channel Attack Exploiting Time-Sliced Scheduling
authors: Hodong Kim and Gyeongsup Lim, Korea University; Seunghee Shin, State University of New York at Binghamton; Youngjoo Shin and Junbeom Hur, Korea University
abstract: Modern GPUs support resource sharing among concurrent applications, introducing the risk of side-channel attacks. While prior research has explored GPU side channels that exploit shared GPU resources, the security implications of time-sliced scheduling, a standard feature for resource sharing in today's GPUs, remain largely unexplored concerning side-channel attacks. In this study, we analyze timing variations caused by concurrent execution under the GPU's time-sliced scheduling mechanism. We begin by identifying the upper bound of a time slice and then leverage this bound to estimate the duration of a concurrent program's time slice, ultimately enabling us to infer the program's overall GPU utilization patterns.
abstract_cn: 现代图形处理器（GPU）支持多个并发应用程序之间共享计算资源，这引入了侧信道攻击的风险。虽然先前的研究已经探索了利用共享GPU资源（如显存、计算单元）的侧信道攻击方法，但对于GPU中另一个标准资源共享特性——时间片调度（time-sliced scheduling）——在侧信道攻击方面的安全影响仍然在很大程度上未被探索。在这项研究中，我们深入分析了GPU时间片调度的时序行为特征，并提出了TIMESLICE-SANDWICH，一种巧妙利用时间片调度机制的GPU侧信道攻击方法。

title: Bridging Usability and Performance: A Tensor Compiler for Autovectorizing Homomorphic Encryption
authors: Edward Chen, Fraser Brown, and Wenting Zheng, Carnegie Mellon University
abstract: Homomorphic encryption (HE) offers strong privacy guarantees by enabling computation over encrypted data. However, the performance of tensor operations in HE is highly sensitive to how the plaintext data is packed into ciphertexts. Large tensor programs introduce numerous possible layout assignments, making it both challenging and tedious for users to manually write efficient HE programs.
abstract_cn: 同态加密技术支持在加密数据上直接进行计算，从而提供了强大的隐私保护能力。然而，同态加密中张量运算的实际执行性能对明文数据如何被打包（packing）到密文中的方式高度敏感。大型张量程序会产生大量可能的布局分配方案，这使得用户手动编写高效的同态加密程序既充满挑战又极为繁琐。本文提出了一种创新的张量编译器框架，有效地弥合了同态加密的可用性与计算性能之间的鸿沟，实现了同态加密张量运算的自动向量化优化。

title: Shadowfax: Hybrid Security and Deniability for AKEMs
authors: Phillip Gajland, IBM Research Europe – Zurich; Vincent Hwang, MPI-SP, Radboud University; Jonas Janneck, Ruhr University Bochum
abstract: As cryptographic protocols transition to post-quantum security, most adopt hybrid solutions combining classical and post-quantum assumptions. This shift often sacrifices efficiency, compactness, or even security. One such property is deniability, which enables users to plausibly deny authorship of potentially incriminating messages. While classical protocols like X3DH key agreement (used in Signal and WhatsApp) provide deniability, post-quantum protocols like PQXDH and Apple's iMessage with PQ3 do not.
abstract_cn: 随着密码协议向后量子安全时代过渡，大多数实际部署采用了结合经典密码假设和后量子密码假设的混合安全方案。然而，这种过渡通常以牺牲效率、协议紧凑性甚至某些安全属性为代价。其中一项容易被牺牲的安全属性是可否认性（deniability），它允许用户合理地否认对潜在敏感消息的 authorship。虽然像Signal协议中使用的X3DH密钥协商协议这样的经典协议能够提供可否认性，但后量子的替代方案尚未能同时提供混合安全性和可否认性。本文提出了Shadowfax，一个同时提供混合安全强度和可否认性的认证密钥封装机制方案。

title: RBOOT: Accelerating Homomorphic Neural Network Inference by Fusing ReLU within Bootstrapping
authors: Zhaomin Yang, Chao Niu, Benqiang Wei, Zhicong Huang, Cheng Hong, and Tao Wei, Ant Group
abstract: A major bottleneck in secure neural network inference using Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) is the evaluation of non-linear activation functions like ReLU, which are inefficient to compute under FHE. State-of-the-art solutions approximate ReLU using high-degree polynomials, incurring significant computational overhead. We present RBOOT, an optimized framework that seamlessly integrates ReLU evaluation into CKKS bootstrapping, significantly reducing multiplication depth and boosting efficiency. Our key insight is that the EvalMod step in CKKS bootstrapping is composed of trigonometric functions, which are nonlinear themselves. Prior works treat bootstrapping and activation functions as independent routines, missing an opportunity to leverage such nonlinearity. By co-optimizing these components, we can exploit such nonlinearity to construct ReLU (and other non-linear functions) within the bootstrapping process itself, greatly reducing the computation overhead. Results on four widely used CNN models show that RBOOT achieves 2.77× faster end-to-end inference and 81% lower memory usage compared to previous polynomial approximation works, while maintaining comparable accuracy.
abstract_cn: 使用全同态加密进行安全神经网络推理的一个主要计算瓶颈是对ReLU等非线性激活函数的求值，因为这些函数在全同态加密下的计算效率极低。当前最先进的解决方案是使用高次多项式来近似ReLU函数，但这会引入非常显著的计算开销。我们提出了RBOOT，一个经过优化的框架，它将ReLU函数的求值无缝地集成到了全同态加密的自举（bootstrapping）过程中，从而大幅加速了同态加密的神经网络推理过程。

title: PANGOLIN: Fuzzing Multilingual IoT Firmware with LLM-Driven Code Analysis
authors: Zhipeng Jia and Xiaokang Yin, Information Engineering University; Shuitao Gan, Laboratory for Advanced Computing and Intelligence Engineering; Chao Zhang, Institute for Network Sciences and Cyberspace, Tsinghua University; JCSS, Tsinghua University (INSC) - Science City (Guangzhou) Digital Technology Group Co., Ltd.; Hangtian Liu, State Key Laboratory of Mathematical Engineering and Advanced Computing; Jiangan Ji, Enzhou Song, and Ruijie Cai, Information Engineering University; Jinglei Tan, State Key Laboratory of Mathematical Engineering and Advanced Computing; Shengli Liu, Information Engineering University
abstract: Multilingual IoT typically refers to the use of multiple languages to implement its web services, such as C, Python, Lua, etc. While some user-accessible interfaces are visualized through the frontend for interaction, a large number of interfaces remain hidden and are not exposed to the frontend in multilingual IoT. Additionally, their parameters often exhibit complex hierarchical structures. Effectively extracting interface specifications from multilingual devices for vulnerability discovery is an urgent problem that remains unresolved. In this paper, we present PANGOLIN, a novel fuzzing solution designed for multilingual IoT devices. First, we utilize LLMs to analyze API dispatching mechanisms and identify interfaces. Then, we introduce an LLM agent to perform cross-language analysis and generate input parameter specifications. Lastly, we utilize response-driven feedback to correct parameter specifications. This knowledge enables semantics-aware fuzzing that can explore deeper code paths and discover more vulnerabilities. PANGOLIN successfully discovered 68 previously unknown vulnerabilities, i.e., 2.96X more than SOTA tool LABRADOR. Notably, 45 of these vulnerabilities were found in hidden interfaces, whereas EAGLEYE was only able to identify 4 such cases. As of the time of writing, all vulnerabilities have been reported to vendors and acknowledged, with 31 vulnerability IDs assigned.
abstract_cn: 多语言物联网通常指的是物联网设备的Web服务后端使用多种编程语言（如C、Python、Lua等）混合实现的情况。虽然一些用户可访问的接口会通过前端页面进行可视化以便用户交互，但在多语言物联网设备中，有大量的内部接口是隐藏的，并不会暴露给前端。此外，这些隐藏接口的参数通常具有复杂的层次化结构。如何有效地对这些隐藏接口进行模糊测试以发现安全漏洞是一个巨大的挑战。本文提出了PANGOLIN，一种创新地利用大型语言模型驱动代码分析来对多语言物联网固件进行高效模糊测试的方法。

title: Patch-Guided Vulnerability Detection: Extracting Java API Security Rules via Attack–Defense Cross-Analysis
authors: Bofei Chen, Shuang Liao, and Lei Zhang, Fudan University; Chibin Zhang and Mathias Payer, EPFL; Yuan Zhang, Fudan University
abstract: Security-sensitive APIs are critical components in modern Java applications, yet improper usage of these APIs frequently leads to severe vulnerabilities such as remote code execution. Existing methods for generating API security rules are limited as they rely on incomplete documentation or infer patterns from source code based on discovered inconsistencies.
abstract_cn: 安全敏感的API是现代Java应用程序中的关键组成部分，然而对这些API的不正确使用经常会导致严重的安全漏洞，例如远程代码执行。现有的API安全规则自动生成方法存在明显的局限性，它们要么依赖不完整的官方文档，要么基于从源代码中发现的不一致模式来推断规则，导致规则覆盖率不足。本文提出了一种全新的补丁引导的漏洞检测方法，通过分析安全补丁的修改模式来自动提取高精度的Java API安全使用规则。

title: The State of Passkeys: Studying the Adoption and Security of Passkeys on the Web
authors: Louis Jannett, Ruhr University Bochum; Andreas Mayer and Maximilian Westers, Heilbronn University of Applied Sciences; Vladislav Mladenov, Ruhr University Bochum; Christian Mainka, University of Wuppertal; Jörg Schwenk, Ruhr University Bochum
abstract: Passkeys provide a secure and phishing-resistant authentication method based on FIDO2 and WebAuthn. They have recently gained popularity, with an increasing number of websites adopting them. Nevertheless, a comprehensive security analysis that evaluates such websites at scale has not been fully addressed. We present PASSKEYS-RADAR, a continuously updated dataset that tracks the deployment of passkeys on the Internet since 2021. To build this dataset, we aggregated diverse sources, including community directories, Tranco 1M, CrUX 18M, and historic Internet archive data. We analyzed the collected data of 872 passkey-enabled websites and shed light on how passkeys are implemented and managed. We identify major differences in how websites allow users to add or delete passkeys and find that websites request authenticators to use deprecated cryptographic algorithms.
abstract_cn: 通行密钥（Passkeys）提供了一种基于FIDO2和WebAuthn标准的安全且抗网络钓鱼的用户认证方法。它们最近获得了广泛的关注和普及，越来越多的网站开始部署通行密钥作为登录方式。然而，大规模评估这些网站的实际部署安全性情况的工作尚未得到充分开展。我们提出了PASSKEYS-RADAR，一个持续更新的数据集，用于大规模跟踪通行密钥在各网站上的部署情况，并对其安全性进行了详细的分析。

title: StackWarp: Breaking AMD SEV-SNP Integrity via Deterministic Stack-Pointer Manipulation through the CPU's Stack Engine
authors: Ruiyi Zhang, Tristan Hornetz, Daniel Weber, Fabian Thomas, and Michael Schwarz, CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security
abstract: Confidential Virtual Machines (CVMs), such as AMD SEV-SNP, aim to protect guest operating systems from an untrusted host by encrypting state and constraining privileged control. These platforms promise isolation even in multi-tenant cloud setups where simultaneous multithreading (SMT) remains enabled. While prior attacks focus on the memory hierarchy or execution units, they largely ignore frontend configurations.
abstract_cn: 机密虚拟机（如AMD的SEV-SNP）旨在通过加密虚拟机状态和限制宿主机特权控制来保护客户操作系统免受不可信云平台宿主的攻击。这些平台承诺在即使启用同步多线程功能的多租户云环境中也能提供强隔离保证。虽然先前的攻击研究主要集中在内存层次结构或执行单元上，但它们在很大程度上忽略了处理器的前端部分——负责指令获取和解码的关键组件。本文提出了StackWarp攻击，一种通过确定性栈指针操作来破坏AMD SEV-SNP机密虚拟机完整性的新型攻击方法。

title: Leveraging Cryptographic Simulator Synthesis for Formally Verifying the FOO E-Voting Protocol
authors: David Baelde, Univ Rennes, CNRS, IRISA; Adrien Koutsos and Justine Sauvage, Inria
abstract: Cryptographic proofs proceed in large part by reductions to cryptographic assumptions expressed as games. These reductions rely on simulators which are often tedious to write and involve a significant amount of trivial code. Thus, simulators are only sketched in pen-and-paper proofs, which is error-prone. Mechanized cryptographic proofs remove the risk of errors, but requiring users to explicitly write simulators is an unreasonable burden.
abstract_cn: 密码学证明在很大程度上是通过将方案的安全性归约到以博弈（game）形式表达的密码学假设来进行的。这些安全性归约依赖于模拟器（simulator）的构造，而模拟器的编写通常非常繁琐且包含大量琐碎的代码。因此，在纸笔证明中，模拟器往往只是被粗略地勾勒出来，这很容易引入人为错误。机械化的密码学证明可以消除人为错误的风险，但要求用户显式地编写完整的模拟器代码。本文提出了一种创新的方法，利用密码学模拟器的自动综合技术来实现密码学证明的形式化验证，大大降低了手动编写模拟器的工作量和出错概率。

title: The Art of Hide and Seek: Making Pickle-Based Model Supply Chain Poisoning Stealthy Again
authors: Tong Liu and Guozhu Meng, Institute of Information Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences; School of Cyber Security, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences; Peng Zhou, Shanghai University; Zizhuang Deng, School of Cyber Science and Technology, Shandong University; State Key Laboratory of Cryptography and Digital Economy Security, Shandong University; Shuaiyin Yao and Kai Chen, Institute of Information Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences; School of Cyber Security, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
abstract: Pickle deserialization vulnerabilities have persisted throughout Python's history, remaining widely recognized yet unresolved. Due to its ability to transparently save and restore complex objects, many AI/ML frameworks continue to adopt pickle as the model serialization protocol despite its inherent risks. As the open-source model ecosystem grows, model-sharing platforms such as Hugging Face have attracted massive participation, significantly amplifying the real-world impact of pickle exploitation and opening new avenues for model supply chain poisoning. Although several state-of-the-art scanners have been developed to detect poisoned models, their incomplete understanding of the poisoning surface allows attackers to bypass them. In this work, we present the first systematic disclosure of the pickle-based model poisoning surface from both model loading and risky function perspectives. Our research demonstrates how pickle-based model poisoning can remain stealthy and highlights critical gaps in current scanning solutions. On the model loading surface, we identify 22 distinct pickle-based model loading paths across five foundational AI/ML frameworks, 19 of which are entirely missed by existing scanners. We further develop a bypass technique named Exception-Directed Programming (EDP) and discover 9 EDP instances, 7 of which can bypass all scanners. On the risky function surface, we discover 133 exploitable gadgets, achieving almost a 100% bypass rate. Even against the best-performing scanner, these gadgets maintain an 89% bypass rate. By systematically revealing the pickle-based model poisoning surface, we achieve practical and robust bypasses against real-world scanners. We responsibly disclose our findings to corresponding vendors, receiving acknowledgments and a $12,000 bug bounty.
abstract_cn: Pickle反序列化漏洞在Python编程语言的整个发展历史中一直是一个长期存在的问题，被广泛认知但始终未能得到根本解决。由于Pickle能够透明地保存和恢复复杂的Python对象而无需额外的序列化代码，许多AI/机器学习框架尽管清楚其固有的安全风险，仍然继续采用Pickle作为模型序列化的标准协议。随着开源模型生态系统的蓬勃发展，像Hugging Face这样的模型共享平台使得模型的上传、下载和分发变得极为便捷和普遍。本文系统研究了基于Pickle的模型供应链中，攻击者如何巧妙地隐藏恶意负载（Hide）以及防御者如何发现这些隐藏攻击（Seek）的攻防技术。

title: B-Privacy: Defining and Enforcing Privacy in Weighted Voting
authors: Samuel Breckenridge, Dani Vilardell, and Andrés Fábrega, Cornell Tech, IC3; Amy Zhao, Ava Labs, IC3; Patrick McCorry, Arbitrum Foundation; Ari Juels, Cornell Tech, IC3
abstract: In traditional, one-vote-per-person voting systems, privacy equates with ballot secrecy: voting tallies are published, but individual voters' choices are concealed.
abstract_cn: 在传统的一人一票投票系统中，隐私的概念等同于选票的保密性：最终的投票统计结果会被公开，但每个投票者的具体选择是隐藏的。然而，在加权投票系统中，不同的投票者拥有不同数量的选票，情况就变得更加复杂。本文提出了B-Privacy框架，系统地定义并研究了加权投票场景中的投票隐私问题。

title: InstrSem: Automatically and Generically Inferring Semantics of (Undocumented) CPU Instructions
authors: Lorenz Hetterich, Fabian Thomas, Tristan Hornetz, and Michael Schwarz, CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security
abstract: Modern CPUs implement complex Instruction Set Architectures (ISAs), yet machine-readable semantics are often incomplete. Worse, many CPUs support undocumented instructions, i.e., bitstrings that execute on hardware but are absent from specifications, leading to potential security vulnerabilities.
abstract_cn: 现代CPU实现了极其复杂的指令集架构，然而机器可读的指令语义描述往往是不完整的。更糟糕的是，许多CPU支持未文档化的指令——即那些实际在硬件上可以执行但在官方规范中完全没有记录的二进制比特串，这些未文档化指令的存在可能导致潜在的安全漏洞。本文提出了InstrSem，一种全新的自动化方法，能够自动且通用地推断包括未文档化指令在内的各种CPU指令的完整语义。

title: Paper Title Under Embargo
authors: Lorenz Hetterich, Tristan Hornetz, Fabian Thomas, and Michael Schwarz, CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security
abstract: This paper is currently under embargo. The final paper PDF and abstract will be available on the first day of the conference.

title: Sliding into the Flight Deck's DMs: Practical Message Attacks on CPDLC
authors: Mehdi Ziazi, ETH Zurich; Khalid Aleem, Independent; Harshad Sathaye, ETH Zurich; Martin Strohmeier, Cyber-Defence Campus, armasuisse Science + Technology
abstract: This paper is currently under embargo, but the paper abstract is available now. The final paper PDF will be available on the first day of the conference.

title: You Know Why, but Still Rely: The Impact of Explainable AI on Trust, Task Load, and Performance in Cybersecurity Decision-Making
authors: Neele Roch, Hannah Sievers, Noé Zufferey, and Verena Zimmermann, ETH Zurich
abstract: With the increasing digitisation of institutions, the demand for effective cybersecurity measures is rising rapidly. Simultaneously, the complexity and volume of cybersecurity tasks are outpacing the capacity of available practitioners. Leveraging AI to augment human cybersecurity expertise has the potential to reduce complexities and cognitive overload. Transparent and human-understandable insights into AI decisions are not only demanded by governance authorities, such as the EU, but also by practitioners themselves when collaborating with AI in high-risk contexts. We report on a between-subjects study (N = 139) that investigated the effects of explainable AI (XAI) explanations on trust, usability, perceived task load, and collaborative task performance among users with cybersecurity domain knowledge in the context of malicious domain blocking. The provision of explanations in this context did not foster trust; in fact, users with domain knowledge reported lower trust after interaction with XAI. Qualitative results suggest that they apply their own decision-making criteria, and that exposing AI decision boundaries may introduce ambiguity and foster mistrust. Although the inclusion of XAI did not increase perceived task load, it also failed to improve performance. These findings raise important questions about the effectiveness of current XAI approaches in knowledge-centric, decision-making settings and underscore the need for more context-sensitive, user-aligned explanation strategies in cybersecurity.
abstract_cn: 随着各类机构的数字化程度不断提高，对有效的网络安全保护措施的需求正在迅速增长。与此同时，网络安全任务的复杂性和任务量正在快速增长，已经超出了现有安全从业人员的能力范围。利用人工智能来增强人类网络安全专家的专业能力，有望有效降低任务的复杂性并减轻认知负荷。透明且人类可理解的AI决策过程可以帮助安全分析师更有效、更信任地与AI系统进行协作。本文通过实证研究系统性地评估了可解释人工智能对安全分析人员在三个维度上的影响：对AI系统的信任程度、任务移交意愿（是否愿意将任务交给AI处理）以及实际的工作绩效变化。

title: Silicon Heist: (Ransom) Attacks for Cloud FPGAs via Privilege Escalation
authors: Simon Klix, Felix Hahn, Maik Ender, Nils Albartus, and Christof Paar, Max Planck Institute for Security and Privacy (MPI-SP); Russell Tessier, University of Massachusetts
abstract: Cloud-based FPGAs have become a billion-dollar industry, allowing users to deploy custom hardware designs with the scalability and flexibility of cloud infrastructure. Running user designs on hardware owned by the cloud service provider (CSP) introduces risks, including intentional hardware damage and Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks against the host. To mitigate these risks, CSPs enforce security mechanisms that restrict user designs and prevent unauthorized behavior. We present a novel privilege escalation path on AMD FPGAs using (i) the Internal Configuration Access Port (ICAP) to circumvent provider defenses, (ii) incrementally escalate attacker capabilities to remote JTAG access, and (iii) investigate the resulting threat vectors.
abstract_cn: 基于云的FPGA（现场可编程门阵列）已经发展成为一个价值数十亿美元的新兴产业，它允许用户利用云基础设施的可扩展性和灵活性来远程部署自定义的硬件加速电路设计。然而，在云服务提供商拥有的物理硬件上运行用户自定义的硬件设计会引入新的安全风险，包括故意损坏硬件设备以及对宿主机发起拒绝服务攻击等。为了降低这些风险，云服务提供商实施了一系列严格的安全隔离机制。本文提出了Silicon Heist，一种通过权限提升对云FPGA进行（勒索软件式）攻击的方法。

title: Bridging Bitcoin to Second Layers via BitVM2
authors: Robin Linus Woll, Stanford University and ZeroSync Association; Lukas Aumayr, University of Edinburgh and Common Prefix; Zeta Avarikioti, TU Wien and Common Prefix; Matteo Maffei, TU Wien; Andrea Pelosi, University of Pisa, University of Camerino, and TU Wien; Orfeas Stefanos Thyfronitis Litos, Imperial College London and Common Prefix; Christos Stefo, TU Wien; David Tse, Stanford University and Byzantine Research; Alexei Zamyatin, BOB
abstract: A holy grail in blockchain infrastructure is a trustless bridge between Bitcoin and its second layers or other chains. We make progress toward this vision by introducing the first light-client-based Bitcoin bridge. At its heart lies BitVM2-CORE, a novel paradigm that enables arbitrary program execution on Bitcoin, combining Turing-complete expressiveness with the security of Bitcoin consensus. BitVM2-BRIDGE advances prior approaches by reducing the trust assumption from an honest majority (t-of-n) to existential honesty (1-of-n) during setup. Liveness is guaranteed with only one rational operator, and any user can act as a challenger, enabling permissionless verification. A production-level implementation of BitVM2 has been developed, and a full challenge verification has been executed on the Bitcoin mainnet.
abstract_cn: 在区块链基础设施领域，一个长期追求的「圣杯」目标是实现比特币与其第二层扩展网络或其他区块链之间的无需信任的资产跨链桥接。我们通过引入首个基于轻客户端的比特币跨链桥接方案，朝这一宏伟愿景迈出了关键的一步。该方案的核心是BitVM2-CORE，一种全新的范式，它将图灵完备的任意程序执行能力与比特币共识协议的安全性创造性地结合在一起，首次实现了在比特币区块链上安全地执行任意复杂程序。

title: "Oh, what people would do with my knife?" Navigating the Dual-Use Dilemma in PoC Exploit Development, Disclosure, and Community Dynamics
authors: Arwa Al Alsadi and Lorenz Kustosch, TU Delft; Lamya Alowain, Independent; Michel Van Eeten and Carlos H. Gañán, TU Delft
abstract: The cybersecurity landscape faces an escalating challenge as proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits transition from demonstrations to weaponized attacks within minutes of disclosure. While research has documented temporal dynamics and malicious deployment, a critical gap remains in understanding the human factors underlying PoC creation. Through semi-structured interviews with 16 PoC developers across diverse regions, we apply Expectancy-Value Theory to reveal PoC development as a complex motivational ecosystem where technical confidence, value assessments, and risk calculations intersect within dual-use tensions. We demonstrate that PoC development spans a continuum from crash demonstrations to weaponized exploits, shaped by multifaceted calculus rather than binary ethics. We identify three theoretical extensions: dual-use moral reasoning enabling responsibility externalization, dynamic value assessment where vendor behavior reshapes disclosure decisions, and identity navigation between ethical research and technical mastery. Vendor responsiveness, community dynamics, and legal constraints significantly influence disclosure strategies. PoC developers adopt risk-mitigation approaches when navigating tensions between security improvement and potential misuse, challenging binary conceptualizations of "responsible" versus "irresponsible" disclosure.
abstract_cn: 网络安全领域正面临着一个日益严峻的挑战：概念验证利用代码在安全漏洞被公开披露后的短短几分钟内，就能从技术演示迅速转化为实际武器化的网络攻击工具。虽然已有研究记录了这种快速武器化过程中的时间动态和恶意部署模式，但在理解驱动安全研究人员创建和发布概念验证利用代码背后的复杂人为因素方面，仍然存在着关键的研究空白。本文通过对来自不同背景的16名概念验证开发者进行深入的半结构化访谈，探讨了安全漏洞研究中的双重使用困境。

title: Lost in Blockchain Address Misuse: Hidden Cross-Platform Risks and Their Security Impact
authors: Zhenzhe Shao, Sun Yat-sen University and Zhejiang University; Jiashuo Zhang, Peking University; Zihao Li, University of Electronic Science and Technology of China and The Hong Kong Polytechnic University; Daoyuan Wu, Lingnan University; Chong Chen and Yiming Shen, Sun Yat-sen University; Lingfeng Bao, Zhejiang University and Hangzhou High-Tech Zone (Binjiang) Institute of Blockchain and Data Security; Yanlin Wang, Sun Yat-sen University; Jiachi Chen, Zhejiang University and Hangzhou High-Tech Zone (Binjiang) Institute of Blockchain and Data Security
abstract: Blockchain systems, such as Ethereum, employ an account-based model, where each account is uniquely identified by an address. As the fundamental interface for user interaction and asset security, addresses are critical but also pose significant risks when misused. In this paper, we systematically reveal and analyze a class of risks termed Address Misuse, which includes two categories: Contract Account (CA) Misuse and Externally Owned Account (EOA) Misuse. Specifically, CA Misuse arises when users mistakenly treat non-contract addresses (NCAs) as CAs, while EOA Misuse occurs when users interact with EOAs whose private keys are exposed. For each category, we reveal the underlying mechanisms and also introduce previously undisclosed attack vectors that enable attackers to exploit these vulnerabilities for profit. To evaluate their prevalence and impacts, we first construct a dataset from GitHub and Stack Exchange, which contains addresses of various blockchain networks. This dataset includes 10 million candidate addresses for misuse analysis and 16 million exposed private keys. We then perform a large-scale on-chain analysis of their associated transactions on Ethereum and BSC. By combining heuristic rules, transaction pattern analysis, and symbolic execution, we identify 65,340 high-risk address instances, with associated asset losses amounting to about 127k ETH and 17.7k BNB, equivalent to over $574.8M. We evaluate the accuracy of our detection methods to ensure the reliability of the results, achieving an overall precision of 99.11%. Besides, our empirical evaluation also reveals two novel, previously undisclosed attack vectors, providing real-world evidence of how attackers actively exploit users' address misuse for profit.
abstract_cn: 区块链系统（例如以太坊）采用基于账户的数据模型，其中每个账户由一个唯一的十六进制地址字符串来标识。作为用户交互和保护数字资产安全的最基本接口，区块链地址的正确使用至关重要——一个字符的错误就可能导致资金的永久损失。本文系统性地揭示并分析了一类我们称之为地址滥用的新型风险，它包括两种主要类型：合约账户地址滥用（即用户错误地将合约账户的地址当作普通外部拥有账户地址来使用，导致资金被锁定在合约中）和外部拥有账户地址滥用（如地址投毒攻击——攻击者生成与受害者常用地址有相同开头和结尾字符的地址来欺骗受害者）。我们的研究发现了这些地址滥用行为如何导致隐藏的跨平台资产损失风险和加密货币盗窃威胁，并量化了其造成的实际经济损失。

title: DDR-SSE: Duplicated Retrieval of Documents for System-wide Secure Searchable Symmetric Encryption
authors: Zichen Gui, University of Georgia, USA; Simon-Philipp Merz and Kenneth G. Paterson, ETH Zürich, Switzerland; Sikhar Patranabis, IBM Research India
abstract: Searchable Symmetric Encryption (SSE) schemes enable efficient keyword searches over encrypted documents at the cost of some leakage. An SSE scheme is said to be system-wide secure if it resists cryptanalysis by an adversary with access to leakage from retrieval of both encrypted indices and encrypted documents. The vast majority of state-of-the-art SSE schemes are, in fact, not system-wide secure (Gui et al., IEEE S&P 2023). Currently, the only efficient system-wide secure SSE scheme is SWiSSSE (Gui et al., PoPETS 2024). However, SWiSSSE requires a client state that is updated per query (which hinders adoption in various practical settings), and its leakage is hard to characterize precisely (thus making security analysis harder).
abstract_cn: 可搜索对称加密方案支持用户对加密存储的文档进行高效的关键词搜索，但这是以会泄露一些额外的信息（称为泄漏）为代价的。如果一个可搜索对称加密方案能够抵抗能够同时访问加密索引和加密文档检索过程中产生的所有泄漏信息的攻击者的密码分析，则该方案被称为具有系统级安全性（system-wide secure）。实际上，绝大多数最先进的可搜索对称加密方案并不具备这种系统级安全性。本文提出了DDR-SSE，一种创新的文档重复检索机制，通过系统化的冗余检索设计来实现系统安全的可搜索加密。

title: Concretely Efficient Blind Signatures Based on VOLE-in-the-Head Proofs and the MAYO Trapdoor
authors: Carsten Baum and Marvin Beckmann, Denmark Technical University; Ward Beullens, IBM, Zürich; Shibam Mukherjee, Graz University of Technology and Know Center, Graz; Christian Rechberger, Graz University of Technology and TACEO, Graz
abstract: Blind signatures (Chaum, CRYPTO 82) are important building blocks in many privacy-preserving applications, such as anonymous credentials or e-cash schemes. Recent years saw a strong interest in building Blind signatures from post-quantum assumptions, primarily from lattices. While performance has improved, no construction has reached practical efficiency in terms of computation and communication. The state of the art requires at least 20 KB size of communication for each showing of a lattice-based Blind signature to a verifier, and more than 100 ms in prover time.
abstract_cn: 盲签名（由Chaum于CRYPTO 82提出）是许多隐私保护应用（如匿名凭证系统和电子现金方案）中的重要密码学构建模块。近年来，人们对于基于后量子安全假设（主要是格密码）来构建盲签名方案产生了浓厚的兴趣。虽然盲签名的性能在持续改善，但目前还没有任何一种构造能够在计算开销和通信开销两个维度上都达到实际可部署的效率水平。本文提出了一种基于VOLE-in-the-Head证明系统的具体高效的盲签名方案。

title: Turn Your Face Into An Attack Surface: Screen Attack Using Facial Reflections in Video Conferencing
authors: Yong Huang, Yanzhao Lu, Mingyang Chen, En Zhang, and Jiazi Li, Zhengzhou University; Wanqing Tu, Durham University
abstract: In video conferencing, human faces serve as the primary visual focal points, playing multifaceted roles that enhance visual communication and emotional connection. However, we argue that a human face is also a side channel, which can unwittingly leak on-screen information through online video feeds. To demonstrate this, we conduct feasibility studies, which reveal that, illuminated by both ambient light and light emitted from displays, the human face can reflect optical variations of different on-screen content. The paper then proposes FaceTell, a novel side-channel attack system that eavesdrops on fine-grained application activities from pervasive yet subtle facial reflections during video conferencing. We implement FaceTell in a real-world testbed with three different brands of laptops and four mainstream video conferencing platforms. FaceTell is then evaluated with 24 human subjects across 13 unique indoor environments. With more than 12 hours of video data, FaceTell achieves a high accuracy of 99.32% for eavesdropping on 28 popular applications and is resilient to many practical impact factors. Finally, potential countermeasures are proposed to mitigate this new attack.
abstract_cn: 在视频会议中，人脸是视觉交流的主要焦点，在增强视觉沟通效果和建立情感连接方面发挥着多方面的作用。然而，我们认为在视频会议场景中，人脸也是一个潜在的安全侧信道，可能会通过在线视频画面无意中泄露屏幕上的敏感信息。为了证明这一威胁的现实性，我们进行了一系列可行性研究，结果表明，在环境光和屏幕光的共同照射下，人脸可以像镜子一样反射屏幕上的内容。本文提出了一种利用人脸面部反射进行屏幕内容窃取的攻击方法。

title: PROBE+DETECT+MITIGATE (PDM): Enabling Cloud Tenants to Self-Defend against Microarchitectural Attacks
authors: Arash Daneshmand, University of British Columbia Okanagan and Concordia University; Hugo Kermabon-Bobinnec, Concordia University; Lingyu Wang, University of British Columbia Okanagan and Concordia University; Makan Pourzandi, Ericsson Security Research, Ericsson Canada; Suryadipta Majumdar, Concordia University; Yosr Jarraya, Ericsson Security Research, Ericsson Canada
abstract: Microarchitectural attacks represent a critical security concern in public cloud environments, as they can cause information leakage between cloud tenants with conflicting interests. Existing solutions usually require provider-level resources, such as hardware performance counters or host processes, which may be inaccessible to cloud tenants. The lack of awareness among cloud tenants may persuade cloud providers to postpone the deployment of vendor patches, as evidenced by patched-yet-active threats, such as PRIME+PROBE and Spectre variants. In this paper, we propose PDM, a solution that enables cloud tenants to independently detect and mitigate microarchitectural attacks without providers' help. First, PDM introduces tenant-based detection based on an interesting observation, i.e., probing the memory space of victim applications using the popular FLUSH+RELOAD attack technique can actually enable detection. Second, PDM achieves efficient tenant-based mitigation by selectively triggering obfuscation and in-memory encryption techniques upon detection. Third, we tackle several key challenges including (i) attacks not involving evictions (e.g., Spectre), (ii) the need for source code or binary instrumentation, (iii) benign noises from the victim or co-resident tenants, and (iv) the tradeoff between accuracy, delay, and overhead. Our experiments show that PDM allows tenants to detect and mitigate various microarchitectural attacks, including PRIME+PROBE and Spectre, in an accurate (e.g., ≥99.72% TPR and ≤0.13% FPR on our testbed, and ≥98.63% TPR and ≤0.83% FPR on AWS Fargate), timely (e.g., 7ms lead time for triggering mitigation), efficient (e.g., ≤2.47% overhead on SPEC CPU 2017), and robust (against both noises and evasive attacks) manner.
abstract_cn: 微架构侧信道攻击——利用CPU等处理器中微架构组件的共享特性（如缓存、分支预测器、预取器等）来窃取其他进程的敏感信息——是公共云计算环境中的一个关键安全威胁。它们可能导致共享同一物理主机的不同云租户之间发生机密信息泄露。现有的防御解决方案通常需要云计算提供商层面的特殊资源支持，例如访问硬件性能计数器或部署宿主机级别的监控进程——这些资源对于普通的云租户来说通常是不可访问或没有权限使用的。云租户对微架构攻击缺乏感知能力会使他们长期暴露在攻击风险之下而浑然不觉。本文提出了一个名为PDM（探测+检测+缓解）的实用框架，使普通的云租户能够不依赖云提供商的任何支持，完全自主地完成对各类微架构攻击的探测（Probe）、检测（Detect）和缓解（Mitigate）。

title: ARM MTE Performance in Practice
authors: Taehyun Noh, University of Texas at Austin; Yingchen Wang, University of California Berkeley; Tal Garfinkel, Google; Mahesh Madhav, Ampere Computing; Daniel Moghimi, Google; Mattan Erez and Shravan Narayan, University of Texas at Austin
abstract: We present the first comprehensive analysis of ARM MTE hardware performance on four different microarchitectures: ARM Big (A7x), Little (A5x), and Performance (Cortex-X) cores on the Google Pixel 8 and Pixel 9, and on Ampere Computing's AmpereOne CPU core. We also include preliminary analysis of MTE on Apple's M5 chip. We investigate performance in MTE's primary application—probabilistic memory safety—on both SPEC CPU benchmarks and in server workloads such as RocksDB, Nginx, PostgreSQL, and Memcached. While MTE often exhibits modest overheads, we also see performance slowdowns up to 6.64× on certain benchmarks. We identify the microarchitectural cause of these overheads and where they can be addressed in future processors. We then analyze MTE's performance for more specialized security applications such as memory tracing, time-of-check time-of-use prevention, sandboxing, and CFI. In some of these cases, MTE offers significant advantages today, while the benefits for other cases are negligible or will depend on future hardware. Finally, we explore where prior work characterizing MTE performance has either been incomplete or incorrect due to methodological or experimental errors.
abstract_cn: 我们首次对ARM MTE（内存标记扩展）硬件特性在四种不同微架构上的实际性能进行了全面深入的分析测试，包括Google Pixel 8和Pixel 9手机上的ARM Big (A7x)核心、Little (A5x)核心和Performance (Cortex-X)核心，以及Ampere Computing公司的AmpereOne服务器CPU核心。我们还包括了对Apple M5芯片上MTE功能的初步性能分析。我们重点研究了MTE在其主要的应用场景——概率性内存安全保护中的实际运行性能表现。

title: Why Johnny Adopts Identity-Based Software Signing: A Usability Case Study of Sigstore
authors: Kelechi G. Kalu, Sofia Okorafor, and Tanmay Singla, Purdue University; Sophie Chen, Carnegie Mellon University; Santiago Torres-Arias and James C. Davis, Purdue University
abstract: Software signing is the most robust method for ensuring the integrity and authenticity of components in a software supply chain. Traditional signing tools burdened practitioners with key management and signer identification, creating both usability challenges and security risks. A new class of next-generation signing tools has automated many of these concerns, but little is known about their usability and its effect on adoption and effectiveness in practice. A usability evaluation can clarify the extent to which next-generation designs succeed and highlight priorities for improvement.
abstract_cn: 软件数字签名是确保软件供应链中各组件完整性和真实性的最可靠方法。传统的软件签名工具给开发运维人员带来了复杂的密钥管理和签名者身份管理负担，这不仅造成了可用性方面的挑战，也引入了新的安全风险。新一代的签名工具已经自动化了其中的许多管理问题，但关于这些新工具在实际开发中的可用性影响和用户采纳情况我们还知之甚少。本文通过一个深入的可用性案例研究，探讨了为什么开发人员会采纳基于身份的软件签名方案。

title: Scribe: Low-memory SNARKs via Read-Write Streaming
authors: Anubhav Baweja, Pratyush Mishra, Tushar Mopuri, Karan Newatia, and Steve Wang, University of Pennsylvania
abstract: Succinct non-interactive arguments of knowledge (SNARKs) enable a prover to produce a short and efficiently verifiable proof of the validity of an arbitrary NP statement. Recent constructions of efficient SNARKs have led to interest in using them for a wide range of applications, but unfortunately, deployment of SNARKs in these applications faces a key bottleneck: SNARK provers require a prohibitive amount of time and memory to generate proofs for even moderately large statements. While there has been progress in reducing prover time, prover memory remains an issue.
abstract_cn: 简洁非交互式知识论证（SNARK）使证明者能够为任意NP语言陈述的有效性生成一个简短的证明，验证者可以高效地验证该证明。最近出现的高效SNARK构造引起了将其广泛应用于各种场景的兴趣，但不幸的是，SNARK在这些应用中的实际部署面临一个关键瓶颈：SNARK的证明者在生成证明时需要消耗过高的内存（例如数百GB），这严重限制了其应用范围。本文提出了Scribe，一种通过创新的读写流式处理架构来实现低内存消耗的SNARK方案。

title: Identifying Provenance of Generative Text-to-Image Models
authors: Anna Yoo Jeong Ha, Wenxin Ding, Stanley Wu, Shawn Shan, Haitao Zheng, and Ben Y. Zhao, University of Chicago
abstract: Fine-tuning provides a fast and cheap way to produce new text-to-image models that are often indistinguishable from ones trained from scratch. Unfortunately, misrepresentation of fine-tuned models creates problems for AI companies and users alike, by disincentivizing competition and misleading users on model quality and ethics of its training process.
abstract_cn: 模型微调提供了一种快速且经济的途径来生成新的文本到图像生成模型，这些微调后的模型通常与从头开始完整训练的模型在生成质量上难以区分。不幸的是，对微调模型的来源进行虚假陈述（例如声称是从头训练的原创模型）会给AI公司和用户都带来严重的问题，这会抑制市场的公平竞争并误导用户对模型质量和训练过程伦理性的判断。本文提出了一种能够有效识别生成式文本到图像模型来源（即判断模型是原创训练还是从其他模型微调而来）的技术方法。

title: Semantics Over Syntax: Uncovering Pre-Authentication 5G Baseband Vulnerabilities
authors: Qiqing Huang and Xingyu Wang, University at Buffalo; Wanda Guo and Guofei Gu, Texas A&M University; Hongxin Hu, University at Buffalo
abstract: Modern 5G user equipment (UE) processes Radio Resource Control (RRC) configuration messages during early control-plane exchanges, before authentication and integrity protection are established. Prior work for testing 5G UEs has largely focused on constructing syntactically invalid inputs. In contrast, we show that syntactically valid but semantically inconsistent messages, which violate specification-level field constraints or cross-field dependencies, can drive baseband implementations into invalid states, triggering assertion failures or modem crashes. These findings reveal semantic inconsistencies in pre-authentication signaling as a critical yet underexplored attack surface in 5G UE implementations. To address this gap, we present Constraint-Guided Semantic Testing (CONSET), a framework that systematically extracts specification-level constraints and leverages them to generate targeted semantic violations for testing 5G UEs. CONSET decodes RRC messages into structured fields, derives schema-based rules, infers cross-field dependencies using a Large Language Model (LLM) in an evidence-bounded manner, and produces syntactically valid test cases that intentionally violate semantic constraints. We evaluate CONSET on both commercial and open-source 5G UEs. On commercial smartphones, it uncovers 7 previously unknown vulnerabilities through responsible disclosure, including 3 high-severity CVEs, affecting 64 chipset models and over 542 commercially available smartphone models. On the open-source OAI UE, CONSET additionally triggers 46 distinct crash sites.
abstract_cn: 现代5G用户设备（UE）在早期控制面信令交互阶段——即在建立网络认证和完整性保护之前——就需要处理无线资源控制（RRC）配置消息。先前对5G用户设备的安全性测试工作主要集中在构造语法上无效的畸形输入来触发设备异常。相比之下，我们证明了即使输入消息在语法上是完全有效的，但如果在语义上与协议规范不一致——即违反了规范中的语义约束但通过了格式检查——同样可能触发不安全的用户设备行为。本文提出了一种超越语法层面的语义分析方法，系统地揭示了预认证阶段5G基带处理中存在的安全漏洞。

title: Window-based Membership Inference Attacks Against Fine-tuned Large Language Models
authors: Yuetian Chen, Yuntao Du, and Kaiyuan Zhang, Purdue University; Ashish Kundu, Cisco Research; Charles Fleming, Cisco Systems; Bruno Ribeiro and Ninghui Li, Purdue University
abstract: Most membership inference attacks (MIAs) against Large Language Models (LLMs) rely on global signals, like average loss, to identify training data. This approach, however, dilutes the subtle, localized signals of memorization, reducing attack effectiveness. We challenge this global-averaging paradigm, positing that membership signals are more pronounced within localized contexts. We introduce WBC (Window-Based Comparison), which exploits this insight through a sliding window approach with sign-based aggregation. Our method slides windows of varying sizes across text sequences, with each window casting a binary vote on membership based on loss comparisons between target and reference models. By ensembling votes across geometrically spaced window sizes, we capture memorization patterns from token-level artifacts to phrase-level structures. Extensive experiments across eleven datasets demonstrate that WBC substantially outperforms established baselines, achieving higher AUC scores and 2–3× improvements in detection rates at low false positive thresholds. Our findings reveal that aggregating localized evidence is fundamentally more effective than global averaging, exposing critical privacy vulnerabilities in fine-tuned LLMs.
abstract_cn: 目前大多数针对大型语言模型的成员推理攻击都依赖于全局性的统计信号（例如模型的平均损失值）来识别训练数据中的成员。然而，这种全局平均的方法会稀释由微调数据引入的微妙的、局部化的记忆信号，从而严重降低攻击的实际效果。我们挑战了这种全局平均的范式，认为成员推理的信号在局部化的上下文窗口中会更加显著。基于这一洞察，我们提出了一种基于窗口的成员推理攻击方法，专门针对经过微调的大型语言模型，通过利用局部化的上下文信息来大幅提高攻击的精确度。

title: Digital Risks and Coping Practices among Roblox Game Creators
authors: Qiurong Song, Rie Helene (Lindy) Hernandez, Xinning Gui, and Yubo Kou, The Pennsylvania State University
abstract: As a growing part of the creator economy, game platforms like Roblox enable millions of users to design, publish, promote, and monetize games. Alongside these opportunities, however, creators on such platforms face significant safety, privacy, and security risks. While prior work has examined online risks for content creators on social media platforms, little is known about the risk landscape of game creators. To address this gap, we interviewed 20 Roblox creators to understand how they perceive, experience, and cope with digital risks.
